I agree with the initial conclusion that macrocephaly is seen in Canavan's disease. This is because Canavan disease involves defective asparaginyl tRNA synthetase activity, resulting in accumulation of N-acetylmethionine, causing cerebral edema and subsequent head growth due to enlarged brain volume—this directly leads to macrocephaly.
